_comment: Research shard for the Alt-Data tab (seed, hand-authored from live MCP pulls on 2026-07-18: CarbonArc US card spend, MetalMiner metal prices, FMP consensus+reported revenue). The refresh routine (scripts/altdata_refresh.md) regenerates/extends this as alt_r0.json..alt_rN.json. Builder overlays these onto the auto-generated defaults by ticker. Numbers here are our own MCP/web reads — no third-party proprietary report text is reproduced.

items.name: Nike
items.narrative: NIKE U.S. card spend (CarbonArc 9-source panel) shows the usual seasonal peaks (back-to-school Jul-Aug, holiday Nov-Dec). Latest full month Jun-26 ~$4.86M, down M/M from May ($5.72M); the Apr-Jun'26 run-rate is broadly in line with Jan-Mar'26 and below the seasonal peaks. Online is ~55% of tracked spend. Consensus revenue for the Sep-26 quarter is $11.38B; Street has modestly under-forecast NIKE the last three prints.

drivers.label: Online ~55% of spend
drivers.note: Physical stabilizing; online carries the seasonality

drivers.label: Jun-26 softer M/M
drivers.note: $4.86M vs May $5.72M; below Jul-Aug & Nov-Dec peaks

sources.name: CarbonArc — US Credit Card Spend (Core Panel)

items.name: Tesla
items.narrative: Consensus revenue for the Jul-26 quarter is $25.99B (reports 2026-07-22). Street accuracy has tightened to ~0.5-1.3% vs reported the last two quarters after a -5.5% miss in Oct-25. Lithium hydroxide (China) is flat at ~EUR17.5k/mt near the low end of its range - soft battery-input pricing consistent with the book's short EV/lithium tilt.

drivers.label: Lithium flat, near lows
drivers.note: LiOH China EUR17.5k/mt at range low - soft input costs

items.name: Albemarle
items.narrative: Lithium hydroxide (China) is flat at ~EUR17.5k/mt, sitting near the low end of its recent range after a prolonged decline - a stabilizing input backdrop for lithium producers. MetalMiner reads the trend as flat/low-volatility. Held long in the book as the primary lithium-price recovery expression.

items.name: Cleveland-Cliffs
items.narrative: Steel HRC (China) is flat at ~CNY3,077/short ton, range-bound with low volatility over the past month. A range-bound Chinese steel complex is consistent with the book's short U.S. flat-rolled steel tilt (CLF / NUE / RS / STLD).

items.name: Affirm
items.narrative: KPI tracked: Total GMV, nowcast from CarbonArc US card-spend + digital-purchase decomposed by merchant partner (large partners vs the long tail). Live consensus pull awaiting (FMP free-tier gated) - the refresh routine fills consensus, Y/Y and the estimate-vs-consensus delta.

drivers.label: Merchant-mix drives GMV
drivers.note: Large partners vs long-tail split is the nowcast core

sources.name: CarbonArc — US card spend / digital purchase

items.name: Chipotle
items.narrative: KPI tracked: same-store sales (SSS) Y/Y, nowcast from CarbonArc US card spend + transactions, with menu-item attach tracking for new LTOs. Live consensus/estimate awaiting the refresh routine.

drivers.label: Card spend + transactions
drivers.note: Traffic vs check split underpins the SSS nowcast

items.name: Lyft
items.narrative: KPI tracked: Gross Bookings, nowcast from a rideshare card-spend/volume panel with market-share tracking vs Uber and Waymo. Live consensus/estimate awaiting the refresh routine.

drivers.label: Share vs Uber / Waymo
drivers.note: City-level share swings drive bookings surprises
